name: Axes
summary: SketchUp's drawing axes consist of three colored lines (red, green, blue), usually perpendicular to each other, displayed in the drawing area. The exception is when the user open an instance with a non-orthogonal transformation. The root model transformation is always orthogonal. The drawing axes are used by drawing tools to align the geometry it creates as well as affecting the inference engine. The plane where the red and green axes lines lie is called the ground plane. The term origin, is used to define the place where all of axes lines start or originate.

methods.name: axes
methods.summary: The axes method returns the vectors representing the directions of the axes.
methods.remarks: The axes method returns the vectors representing the directions of the axes.

returns.doc: Array - an array of three vectors.

methods.name: origin
methods.summary: The #origin method returns the origin of the axes.
methods.remarks: The #origin method returns the origin of the axes.

methods.name: set
methods.summary: The #set method allows the axes to be manipulated.
methods.remarks: The #set method allows the axes to be manipulated. The axes must always be orthogonal, otherwise an error is thrown.

params.name: xaxis
params.doc: Vector3d - The x axis to set.

params.name: yaxis
params.doc: Vector3d - The y axis to set.

params.name: zaxis
params.doc: Vector3d - The z axis to set.

methods.name: sketch_plane
methods.summary: The sketch_plane method returns a plane representing the ground plane of the axes.
methods.remarks: The sketch_plane method returns a plane representing the ground plane of the axes.

returns.doc: Array - of 4 numbers that give the coefficients of a plane equation.

methods.name: to_a
methods.summary: The axes method returns the origin and vectors representing the axes.
methods.remarks: The axes method returns the origin and vectors representing the axes.

returns.doc: Array - an array of a point and three vectors.

methods.name: transformation
methods.summary: The #transformation method returns the transformation of the axes.
methods.remarks: The #transformation method returns the transformation of the axes. This is useful when creating tools that respect the model's drawing axes.

methods.name: xaxis
methods.summary: The #xaxis method returns the x axis of the axes.
methods.remarks: The #xaxis method returns the x axis of the axes.

returns.doc: the x axis for the axes.

methods.name: yaxis
methods.summary: The #yaxis method returns the y axis of the axes.
methods.remarks: The #yaxis method returns the y axis of the axes.

returns.doc: the y axis for the axes.

methods.name: zaxis
methods.summary: The #zaxis method returns the z axis of the axes.
methods.remarks: The #zaxis method returns the z axis of the axes.

returns.doc: the z axis for the axes.
